findings.title: ingestcli now requires external metrics initialization before first request
findings.why: The new `Observe` calls assume `ingestmetric.WriteAPIDuration` and `ingestmetric.IngestAPIDuration` are already populated, but `ingestmetric` itself never initializes them. That adds a hidden init-order requirement to an existing exported package.
findings.risk_if_unchanged: Any consumer or focused test that uses `pkg/ingestor/ingestcli` without importing `pkg/metrics` first will panic on the first write or ingest call. That is a backward-compatibility break for the package and makes initialization order part of the runtime contract.
findings.evidence: `WriteAPIDuration` and `IngestAPIDuration` are package-level `prometheus.Observer` globals with zero-value nil interfaces in `pkg/ingestor/ingestmetric/metric.go`, and the only initializer is `ingestmetric.InitIngestMetrics()` from `pkg/metrics.InitMetrics()`. The new defers in `startChunkedHTTPRequest` and `Ingest` call `Observe(...)` unconditionally, so there is no local guard when `pkg/metrics` was never imported.
findings.change_request: Can we make `ingestcli` safe without depending on `pkg/metrics` side effects, for example by initializing default observers inside `ingestmetric` or by explicitly initializing the histogram before use? Please add a focused test for the no-`pkg/metrics` import path so this backward-compatibility contract stays covered.
